slides.heading: Hey! Welcome 👋
slides.body:
You don't need to be a "tech person" to start this.

All you need is a little curiosity — and you already have that, because you're here!

We're going to explore what's inside a computer, one tiny step at a time. No jargon. No rushing. Just you and the machine getting to know each other.

slides.heading: You already use a computer every day
slides.body:
You've opened apps, played music, visited websites, saved photos.

But here's a question — who decides which app gets to run when you click it? Who makes sure the music keeps playing while you browse? Who keeps your files safe?

Something is managing all of that, quietly, in the background.
bullets: 📱 Every tap on your phone is being managed
🎵 Music keeps playing while you switch apps
📂 Files stay safe even when you close them
🤔 Something is doing all this — but what?

slides.heading: There's a quiet manager running your computer
slides.body:
That quiet manager is called an Operating System — or OS for short.

Think of it like the manager of a busy restaurant. Chefs need the oven, the fridge, the counter space. If everyone just grabbed whatever they wanted, it would be chaos.

The manager says: "You use the oven now. You wait your turn. The fridge is yours for 2 minutes."

Your OS does the exact same thing — but for apps, instead of chefs.
bullets: 🍽️ Apps are like chefs — they all need resources
🏨 The OS is the manager who coordinates everything
⚖️ It keeps things fair and prevents crashes
💻 On your computer, this OS is probably Windows, Mac, or Linux

slides.heading: Linux is one of those managers — and you can peek inside it
slides.body:
Windows and Mac are operating systems too — but they are closed boxes. You can use them, but you can't see how they work or change them.

Linux is different. It's open. You can look inside, change things, and learn exactly how it works.

That's why we're using Linux to learn. It's like learning to cook with a glass oven — you can see everything happening inside.
bullets: 🔒 Windows & Mac: closed — hard to peek inside
🪟 Linux: open — you can see and change everything
📚 Great for learning how computers really work
🚀 Used by phones, websites, satellites, and more
